rect.data-narration: Quality Assurance (QA) and Quality Control (QC) are essential for delivering high-quality software products.

text.data-narration: Quality Assurance (QA) and Quality Control (QC) are essential for delivering high-quality software products.

line.data-narration: Activities performed throughout the software development life cycle to ensure software quality.
Activities performed after the software development life cycle to ensure software quality.

rect.data-narration: The Single Responsibility Principle (SRP) states that a class should have only one reason to change.

text.data-narration: The Single Responsibility Principle (SRP) states that a class should have only one reason to change.

line.data-narration: The Open-Closed Principle (OCP) states that a class should be open for extension but closed for modification.
The Interface Segregation Principle (ISP) states that clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they do not use.

rect.data-narration: Unit testing, integration testing, system testing, and acceptance testing are essential for ensuring software quality.

text.data-narration: Unit testing, integration testing, system testing, and acceptance testing are essential for ensuring software quality.

line.data-narration: Test-driven development (TDD), behavior-driven development (BDD), and code reviews are essential for ensuring software quality.
Pair programming, static code analysis, and inspection are essential for ensuring software quality.
